extra.reasoning: The mutual-exclusion property NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) is violated. rec_drug_a is set true only by rule rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50) when hyperkalemia AND bradycardia AND low_ejection_fraction hold. rec_drug_b is set true by rule rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50) whenever hyperkalemia holds. No rule ever sets either output to false (the default only applies when an output is left unset), and there are no higher-priority overriding rules. Therefore, on the input hyperkalemia=true, bradycardia=true, low_ejection_fraction=true, both rules fire: rec_drug_a=true and rec_drug_b=true, making the forbidden conjunction true. The rec_drug_c rules (ckd_dialysis, pregnancy, seizure_history) are irrelevant to this property, so those inputs are set false in the minimal witness. In fact, hyperkalemia is a necessary condition for rec_drug_a, and hyperkalemia alone forces rec_drug_b, so any state with rec_drug_a true necessarily has rec_drug_b true — the property fails on the entire rec_drug_a region.
